title: Mutation-Seam Custom-Domain DNS Closure And Operations-Web Env Realignment
objective: Close the residual hosted custom-domain lane by saving the authoritative GoDaddy CNAME, proving `https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` live over HTTPS, realigning `apex-operations-web` back to that custom-domain seam target across environments, and rerunning governed public proof after the final production cutover.
decision_rationale: Packet 033 left one narrow residue rather than a functional blocker: the hosted PM lane was already green through the Render hostname, so the smallest truthful next slice was to finish the intended custom-domain path instead of reopening source work or generic platform exploration.

The decisive cutover checks were public DNS resolution and live HTTPS behavior at `mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com`; the Render settings page briefly lagged with stale waiting labels during propagation, so runtime truth had to be established from the live endpoint and repo-owned smoke rather than from dashboard text alone.

Because `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL` is deployment-scoped on Vercel, the environment realignment required a fresh preview-derived deployment and then a new production promotion before the public browser host could truthfully be said to target the intended custom-domain seam host.
execution_summary: Completed the GoDaddy DNS write for `mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` by saving the CNAME `mutation-seam -> apex-platform-mutation-seam.onrender.com` through the account OTP flow, then confirmed public resolution through both `8.8.8.8` and `1.1.1.1` before the local resolver caught up.

Proved that `https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` was the real live seam endpoint by loading `/health` successfully in the browser and rerunning `apps/mutation-seam/scripts/smoke_deployed_mutation_seam.py` against the custom domain to `RESULT PASS`.

Realigned `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL` on the real Vercel project `apex-operations-web` back to `https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` across Production, Preview, and Development using the isolated link at `C:/APEX Platform/tmp/vercel-apex-operations-web-link`, then verified the effective values with `vercel env pull`.

Created a fresh preview-derived deployment `https://apex-operations-4f2acc4np-jasonlswenson-sys-projects.vercel.app` and promoted it to new production deployment `dpl_8kQsnU68Jjej285HbWpEEdRVHDZv` at `https://apex-operations-pp62b0s3o-jasonlswenson-sys-projects.vercel.app`, now aliased to `https://operations.apexpowerops.com`.

Revalidated the public same-origin PM routes and reran the governed promoted-host proof after the final cutover, closing again at `PROMOTED_HOST_SUMMARY failed=0` with the intended custom-domain seam target in place.

validation.godaddy_dns_commit: GoDaddy DNS UI -> PASS (success alert `Your DNS record has been updated successfully` after saving CNAME `mutation-seam -> apex-platform-mutation-seam.onrender.com`)
validation.public_dns_resolution: `Resolve-DnsName mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com -Server 8.8.8.8` and `-Server 1.1.1.1` -> PASS (CNAME chain resolves through `apex-platform-mutation-seam.onrender.com` to the Render origin); later the default resolver also resolved the same chain
validation.vercel_env_realignment: `vercel env pull` for Production, Preview, and Development from `C:/APEX Platform/tmp/vercel-apex-operations-web-link` -> PASS (`MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL="https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com"` in all three environments)
validation.production_cutover: `npx -y vercel redeploy https://apex-operations-71q36o7vp-jasonlswenson-sys-projects.vercel.app --target preview --scope jasonlswenson-sys-projects` -> PASS (`https://apex-operations-4f2acc4np-jasonlswenson-sys-projects.vercel.app`); `npx -y vercel promote https://apex-operations-4f2acc4np-jasonlswenson-sys-projects.vercel.app --yes --scope jasonlswenson-sys-projects` -> PASS (created production deployment `dpl_8kQsnU68Jjej285HbWpEEdRVHDZv`, aliased to `https://operations.apexpowerops.com`)
validation.public_same_origin_routes: direct probes against production alias -> PASS (`/api/v1/reads/approval-queue`, `/api/v1/schedule/projects`, `/api/v1/schedule/drivers`, `/api/v1/schedule/tracer?task_id=probe-task`, `/api/v1/schedule/variance` all returned `200 application/json`)
validation.promoted_host_proof: `npx pnpm --dir C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform --filter @apex/operations-web smoke:promoted-host --operations-web-base-url https://operations.apexpowerops.com --control-plane-base-url https://control.apexpowerops.com --skip-authenticated-checks` -> PASS (`PROMOTED_HOST_SUMMARY failed=0`)

next_packet_candidate: None - hosted mutation-seam custom-domain lane closed
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-06 as the residual custom-domain closure packet. Packet 034 truthfully closes the hosted mutation-seam lane end-to-end: authoritative DNS is present, the intended custom-domain seam host is healthy, `operations-web` production is back on a deployment built with `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL=https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com`, and the governed public proof suite closes green. Render settings text lagged during propagation but no longer defined runtime truth.
next_action: No immediate follow-on packet is required for this hosted custom-domain lane. Reopen only for a new bounded infrastructure objective or a production regression.
